title: 70% of all crypto wrench attacks happen in France: Report
summary: Centralized data collection is a honeypot for hackers and organized criminals looking to target crypto holders and their families, according to Bitcoiners.

title: Analysts forecast a Bitcoin crash to the $60K level, revisiting 2026 low
summary: The price of Bitcoin is about $75,800 at the time of publication, a nearly 40% decrease from the all-time high of about $126,000 reached in October 2025.

title: China Coal Mine Blast Tests Limits of Xi’s Energy Security Push
summary: China’s world-beating coal production has helped shield its economy from the worst of the Iran war shock. Now the deadliest mining disaster in years is raising uncomfortable questions about the cost of that drive.

title: LNG Tanker Exits Hormuz for India for First Time Since War Began
summary: A liquefied natural gas tanker carrying a shipment for India has exited the Strait of Hormuz, the first for the country from the Persian Gulf since the Iran war began months ago as the region’s exporters discreetly supply key buyers.

title: Firefox's Big Redesign Gives You a Button to Kill All the AI
summary: Project Nova is coming later this year with a cleaner look, compact mode, and a toggle to make AI features disappear entirely.

title: Bitcoin Dives Below $75K for First Time in a Month as Crypto Liquidations Near $1 Billion
summary: Bitcoin touched its lowest price in a month overnight following an awful week for ETFs, which shed over $1.25 billion this week.

title: ECB warns EU finance ministers that easing euro stablecoin rules would weaken banks: Reuters
summary: ECB President Christine Lagarde warned the changes would destabilize bank funding and weaken interest-rate transmission.

title: FTX’s former law firm, auditor agree to pay $66 million to settle customer claims over fraud
summary: The law firm, Fenwick & West, denied wrongdoing and still faces a separate $525 million suit in Washington that is not covered by Friday's deal.
